source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
title: Minimize user access to Container Image repositories

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
input_row.title: Minimize user access to Container Image repositories
input_row.description: Note: GCR is now deprecated, see the references for more details. Restrict user access to GCR or AR, limiting interaction with build images to only authorized personnel and service accounts.
input_row.rationale: Weak access control to GCR or AR may allow malicious users to replace built images with vulnerable or back-doored containers. Impact: Care should be taken not to remove access to GCR or AR for accounts that require this for their operation. Any account granted the Storage Object Viewer role at the project level can view all objects stored in GCS for the project.
input_row.audit: For Images Hosted in AR: 1. Go to Artifacts Browser by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/artifacts 2. From the list of artifacts select each repository with format Docker 3. Under the Permissions tab, review the roles for each member and ensure only authorized users have the Artifact Registry Administrator, Artifact Registry Reader, Artifact Registry Repository Administrator and Artifact Registry Writer roles. Users may have permissions to use Service Accounts and thus Users could inherit privileges on the AR repositories. To check the accounts that could do this: 1. Go to IAM by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/iam 2. Apply the filter Role: Service Account User. Note that other privileged project level roles will have the ability to write and modify AR repositories. Consult the GCP CIS benchmark and IAM documentation for further reference. Using Command Line: gcloud artifacts repositories get-iam-policy <repository-name> --location <repository-location> The output of the command will return roles associated with the AR repository and which members have those roles. For Images Hosted in GCR: Using Google Cloud Console: GCR bucket permissions 1. Go to Storage Browser by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser 2. From the list of storage buckets, select artifacts.<project_id>.appspot.com for the GCR bucket 3. Under the Permissions tab, review the roles for each member and ensure only authorized users have the Storage Admin, Storage Object Admin, Storage Object Creator, Storage Legacy Bucket Owner, Storage Legacy Bucket Writer and Storage Legacy Object Owner roles. Users may have permissions to use Service Accounts and thus Users could inherit privileges on the GCR Bucket. To check the accounts that could do this: 1. Go to IAM by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/iam 2. Apply the filter Role: Service Account User. Note that other privileged project level roles will have the ability to write and modify objects and the GCR bucket. Consult the GCP CIS benchmark and IAM documentation for further reference. Using Command Line: To check GCR bucket specific permissions gsutil iam get gs://artifacts.<project_id>.appspot.com The output of the command will return roles associated with the GCR bucket and which members have those roles. Additionally, run the following to identify users and service accounts that hold privileged roles at the project level, and thus inherit these privileges within the GCR bucket: gcloud projects get-iam-policy <project_id> \ --flatten="bindings[].members" \ --format='table(bindings.members,bindings.role)' \ --filter="bindings.role:roles/storage.admin OR bindings.role:roles/storage.objectAdmin OR bindings.role:roles/storage.objectCreator OR bindings.role:roles/storage.legacyBucketOwner OR bindings.role:roles/storage.legacyBucketWriter OR bindings.role:roles/storage.legacyObjectOwner" The output from the command lists the service accounts that have create/modify permissions. Users may have permissions to use Service Accounts and thus Users could inherit privileges on the GCR Bucket. To check the accounts that could do this: gcloud projects get-iam-policy <project_id>  \ --flatten="bindings[].members" \ --format='table(bindings.members)' \ --filter="bindings.role:roles/iam.serviceAccountUser" Note that other privileged project level roles will have the ability to write and modify objects and the GCR bucket. Consult the GCP CIS benchmark and IAM documentation for further reference.
input_row.remediation: For Images Hosted in AR: Using Google Cloud Console: 1. Go to Artifacts Browser by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/artifacts 2. From the list of artifacts select each repository with format Docker 3. Under the Permissions tab, modify the roles for each member and ensure only authorized users have the Artifact Registry Administrator, Artifact Registry Reader, Artifact Registry Repository Administrator and Artifact Registry Writer roles. Using Command Line: gcloud artifacts repositories set-iam-policy <repository-name> <path-to- policy-file> --location <repository-location> To learn how to configure policy files see: https://cloud.google.com/artifact- registry/docs/access-control#grant For Images Hosted in GCR: Using Google Cloud Console: To modify roles granted at the GCR bucket level: 1. Go to Storage Browser by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser. 2. From the list of storage buckets, select artifacts.<project_id>.appspot.com for the GCR bucket 3. Under the Permissions tab, modify permissions of the identified member via the drop-down role menu and change the Role to Storage Object Viewer for read- only access. For a User or Service account with Project level permissions inherited by the GCR bucket, or the Service Account User Role: 1. Go to IAM by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/iam 2. Find the User or Service account to be modified and click on the corresponding pencil icon. 3. Remove the create/modify role (Storage Admin / Storage Object Admin / Storage Object Creator / Service Account User) on the user or service account. 4. If required add the Storage Object Viewer role - note with caution that this permits the account to view all objects stored in GCS for the project. Using Command Line: To change roles at the GCR bucket level: Firstly, run the following if read permissions are required: gsutil iam ch <type>:<email_address>:objectViewer gs://artifacts.<project_id>.appspot.com Then remove the excessively privileged role (Storage Admin / Storage Object Admin / Storage Object Creator) using: gsutil iam ch -d <type>:<email_address>:<role> gs://artifacts.<project_id>.appspot.com where: • <type> can be one of the following: o user, if the <email_address> is a Google account. o serviceAccount, if <email_address> specifies a Service account. o <email_address> can be one of the following: ▪ a Google account (for example, someone@example.com). ▪ a Cloud IAM service account. To modify roles defined at the project level and subsequently inherited within the GCR bucket, or the Service Account User role, extract the IAM policy file, modify it accordingly and apply it using: gcloud projects set-iam-policy <project_id> <policy_file> Default Value: By default, GCR is disabled and access controls are set during initialisation. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/container-registry/docs/ 2. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/service-accounts 3. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/iam 4. https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/access-control#grant
input_row.impact: Care should be taken not to remove access to GCR or AR for accounts that require this for their operation. Any account granted the Storage Object Viewer role at the project level can view all objects stored in GCS for the project.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control can be fully verified using the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud), the GCP CLI, and GCP APIs through predefined commands for both Artifact Registry (AR) and Google Container Registry (GCR). The presence of specific IAM roles related to access control can be programmatically assessed via these tools, ensuring that only authorized users have the requisite permissions.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_artifact_registry_access_control_minimized

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP APIs/Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) Methods to Use:**
  - For Artifact Registry (AR): Use `gcloud artifacts repositories get-iam-policy <repository-name> --location <repository-location>` to retrieve the IAM policy attached to each repository. Validate that only authorized users have the `Artifact Registry Administrator`, `Artifact Registry Reader`, `Artifact Registry Repository Administrator`, and `Artifact Registry Writer` roles.
  
  - For Google Container Registry (GCR): Use `gsutil iam get gs://artifacts.<project_id>.appspot.com` to check the IAM roles associated with the GCR bucket. Confirm that permissions like `Storage Admin`, `Storage Object Admin`, `Storage Object Creator`, `Storage Legacy Bucket Owner`, `Storage Legacy Bucket Writer`, and `Storage Legacy Object Owner` are appropriately restricted.

- **GCP CLI Commands:**
  - To check project-level roles that might affect AR and GCR: 
    ```bash
    gcloud projects get-iam-policy <project_id> \
    --flatten="bindings[].members" \
    --format='table(bindings.members,bindings.role)' \
    --filter="bindings.role:roles/storage.admin OR bindings.role:roles/storage.objectAdmin OR bindings.role:roles/storage.objectCreator OR bindings.role:roles/storage.legacyBucketOwner OR bindings.role:roles/storage.legacyBucketWriter OR bindings.role:roles/storage.legacyObjectOwner"
    ```

- **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
  - Enumerate all repositories hosted in Artifact Registry and GCR within the project scope.
  
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - Pass: Only authorized accounts (e.g., specific service accounts, admin accounts as per org policy) hold the roles specified for AR or GCR.
  - Fail: Unauthorized roles/users found in the IAM policy that could lead to improperly managed or malicious access to container images.

- **Expected Values:**
  - Ensure that storage and artifact roles are limited and specific accounts are validated against known authorized personnel.

This automation ensures compliance with the principle of least privilege, protecting container image repositories from unauthorized or harmful alterations.
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. Both Artifact Registry (AR) and Google Container Registry (GCR) IAM policies can be programmatically enumerated and analyzed using GCP APIs, Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`), and CLI tools (`gsutil` for GCR). No manual review is strictly required unless the list of "authorized" users is not programmatically defined.
- **GCP SDK Capabilities:**  
  The described approach leverages `gcloud artifacts repositories get-iam-policy` for AR and `gsutil iam get` for GCR, both of which are valid and supported. Project-level IAM can also be checked via `gcloud projects get-iam-policy`.
- **CloudFormation/GCP Config:**  
  CloudFormation is AWS-specific and not relevant here. GCP Config (Config Connector, Config Validator) could be used for policy-as-code enforcement, but for audit/assessment, the described approach is sufficient.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 8/10**

- **Automation Details:**  
  - **Artifact Registry:**  
    - `gcloud artifacts repositories get-iam-policy <repository-name> --location <repository-location>` is correct.
    - Enumerating all repositories can be done with `gcloud artifacts repositories list`.
  - **GCR:**  
    - `gsutil iam get gs://artifacts.<project_id>.appspot.com` is correct for GCR buckets.
    - Note: GCR is deprecated, but still widely used; AR is preferred.
  - **Project-level IAM:**  
    - The provided `gcloud projects get-iam-policy` command is technically correct and filters for relevant roles.
- **Program Name:**  
  - `gcp_artifact_registry_access_control_minimized` is descriptive, but could be more explicit (see Improvements).
- **Missing Details:**  
  - The assessment does not specify how to enumerate all AR repositories or GCR buckets, which is necessary for full automation.
  - No mention of service account analysis (e.g., ensuring only specific service accounts have access).
  - No explicit mapping of "authorized" users—this must be defined for automation to be meaningful.
  - No reference to organization policies (Org Policy Constraints) that could enforce or restrict access at a higher level.
- **Manual Steps:**  
  - Not required unless the list of authorized users is not machine-readable.

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Enumerate All Resources:**  
  - For AR: Use `gcloud artifacts repositories list --location=<location>` to enumerate all repositories, then iterate over each for IAM policy.
  - For GCR: GCR buckets follow a naming pattern, but projects may have multiple GCR buckets (multi-region). Consider enumerating all buckets with `gsutil ls` and filtering for GCR patterns.
- **Authorized Users Source:**  
  - Define how the list of "authorized" users/service accounts is sourced—ideally from a central IAM group, config file, or org policy.
- **Service Account Analysis:**  
  - Explicitly check for service accounts with elevated access and ensure they are expected.
- **Org Policy Constraints:**  
  - Consider referencing org policy constraints (e.g., `constraints/gcp.restrictNonAdminServiceAccountRoleGrant`) if relevant.
- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  - More explicit:  
    `gcp_artifact_registry_gcr_minimal_user_access`  
    or  
    `gcp_artifact_registry_gcr_iam_least_privilege`
- **Alternative API Methods:**  
  - For large orgs, consider using the [Cloud Asset Inventory API](https://cloud.google.com/asset-inventory/docs/reference/rest) to enumerate IAM policies across all resources.
- **Documentation:**  
  - Reference the deprecation of GCR and migration to AR in the control logic.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The assessment is technically sound and feasible with GCP APIs and SDKs.
- Minor improvements are needed for completeness and clarity, but the core automation is valid.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **MODIFY**

- **Keep the automated approach,** but **modify** the automation details to:
  - Explicitly enumerate all AR repositories and GCR buckets.
  - Define the source of "authorized" users/service accounts.
  - Consider org policy constraints if relevant.
  - Update the program name for clarity.
  - Optionally, reference Cloud Asset Inventory API for large-scale audits.

---

**Summary Table:**

| Section                | Review Outcome         |
|------------------------|-----------------------|
| Approach Validation    | AGREE                 |
| Technical Accuracy     | 8/10                  |
| Improvements           | See above             |
| Confidence             | HIGH                  |
| Final Recommendation   | MODIFY (see above)    |

---

**Example Improved Program Name:**  
`gcp_artifact_registry_gcr_iam_least_privilege`

**Example Improved Automation Steps:**  
1. Enumerate all AR repositories:  
   `gcloud artifacts repositories list --format="value(name,location)"`
2. For each repository, get IAM policy:  
   `gcloud artifacts repositories get-iam-policy <repo> --location <loc>`
3. Enumerate GCR buckets:  
   `gsutil ls | grep artifacts.<project_id>.appspot.com`
4. For each bucket, get IAM policy:  
   `gsutil iam get gs://artifacts.<project_id>.appspot.com`
5. Compare all principals with a pre-defined list of authorized users/service accounts.
6. Flag any unauthorized access.

---
